Job Summary:The Risk Officer will be responsible for identifying, assessing, monitoring, and mitigating risks associated with the business activities of Nanhua Singapore Pte. Ltd. This role requires close collaboration with trading desks, compliance teams, senior management, and other stakeholders to ensure that risks related to market activities, operational procedures, regulatory requirements, and counterparty exposures are appropriately managed.Key Responsibilities:Risk Identification and Assessment:Conduct thorough risk assessments related to the trading of derivatives, including market risk, credit risk, liquidity risk, operational risk, and counterparty risk.Identify and evaluate emerging risks in the derivatives market and financial services industry, staying up-to-date with market conditions and regulatory changes.Work closely with trading desks to assess risk exposure in relation to trading positions, strategies, and market conditions.Risk Monitoring and Reporting:Monitor risk limits, thresholds, and adherence to risk management policies in real time, ensuring that all risk parameters for trading activities are maintained within acceptable levels.Prepare and present regular risk reports to senior management, the risk committee, and regulators (if applicable), highlighting key risk exposures, potential breaches, and mitigation strategies.Oversee the development and maintenance of risk dashboards and reporting tools to ensure that risk data is accurate, timely, and actionable.Compliance and Regulatory Risk Management:Ensure compliance with the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) regulations, particularly the Securities and Futures Act (SFA) and other relevant regulatory requirements specific to derivatives trading.Liaise with compliance officers to ensure that risk management policies are aligned with regulatory expectations, including adherence to trading conduct rules, margin requirements, and reporting obligations.Assist in the preparation of regulatory filings, audits, and responses to regulatory inquiries related to risk management.Stress Testing and Scenario Analysis:Conduct stress testing and scenario analysis to assess the potential impact of adverse market events, including significant market movements, liquidity disruptions, and financial crises on the firm's derivatives trading positions.Develop and maintain stress test models, ensuring they reflect realistic market conditions and risk factors relevant to the derivatives market.Risk Governance and Policies:Contribute to the development and implementation of risk management frameworks, policies, and procedures to ensure sound risk governance within the organization.Participate in the development and periodic review of risk limits, thresholds, and risk appetite statements.Ensure that risk management policies are regularly updated in accordance with industry best practices, regulatory changes, and the evolving business environment.Ad-Hoc Risk Projects:Lead or participate in ad-hoc risk management projects as required, such as system implementations, new product launches, or changes to trading strategies.Assist in evaluating and managing risk during the introduction of new derivatives products or trading strategies.Experience:Minimum 2-3 years of experience in risk management, preferably within the capital markets or derivatives trading space.In-depth knowledge of derivatives products, including options, futures and leveraged foreign exchange.Familiarity with the regulatory environment in Singapore (MAS guidelines, SFA, and other relevant regulations).Experience in risk modeling, stress testing, and scenario analysis is highly preferred.

Company description:The Population Health Campus (PHC) under the National Healthcare Group (NHG) leads the development of population health for 1.5 million residents of Central and North Singapore. Through its three missions - Population Health, Community Care, Primary Care, PHC plays an integral role in joining up care, and transforming preventive care and aged care. In close collaboration with health and social care partners such as hospitals, specialty centres, general practitioners, and community care providers, PHC aims to keep residents well and healthy, and empower them to care for their own wellbeing. PHC network of care consists of NHG Polyclinics, Central-North Primary Care Network, NHG Community Health Teams, NHG Preventive Health Centre, NHG Telehealth & Call Centre, NHG Digital Health Services, NHG Pharmacy, and NHG Diagnostics. Together with our partners and residents, we are committed to the grand challenge of adding 15 million years of healthy life for all in Central and North Singapore.Job description:The Senior Executive of Population Health Office's Development and Innovation (Planning and Development) will support the Manager in driving development and innovation across the Population Health Campus (PHC). This includes planning for capacity, capabilities and manpower development at the system level, and enabling innovation in services and programmes in both the primary care and community care settings. These efforts will strategically position PHC to achieve its long-term aspirations and deliver on its population health mandate for residents in Central and North Singapore.  Major Duties & Responsibilities Support the development and implementation of CHI@PHC to drive and enable innovation in services and programmes across PHC. This involves establishing resources both internally and with the broader innovation ecosystem, building capability for innovation and nurturing a robust innovation culture.  Establish baselines, and assess and identify gaps and opportunities, in alignment with PHC's long term goals. Formulate plans for capacity, capabilities, and manpower development at the system level, for PHC to achieve its long-term strategic goals and priorities. Commission and facilitate initiatives for development and innovation in both primary care and community care settings. This requires close collaboration with cross-functional teams and clinician partners. Support in the establishment of evaluation metrics and frameworks to assess effectiveness of development and innovation initiatives. Implement the framework to evaluate and monitor performance of development and innovation initiatives and size up the system impact across PHC.  Job Requirements Good bachelor's degree in any discipline. Having healthcare operations/planning experience will be advantageous. Must possess strong administrative and project coordination skills. Strong written, analytical and communication skills. Proficient in various computer applications, including Microsoft Word, Excel & PowerPoint Independent, self-motivated and result-oriented Good communication, collaboration, and problem-solving skills. Resourceful and able to work in ambiguous situation with a can-do attitude. Attention to detail and able to multi-task and manage various project elements and timelines.

Company description:The National Healthcare Group (NHG) is a leader in public healthcare in Singapore, recognised at home and abroad for the quality of its medical expertise and facilities. Care is provided through an integrated network of seven primary care polyclinics, acute care and tertiary hospitals, national specialty centres and business divisions. Together they bring a rich legacy of medical expertise to our philosophy of integrated patient-centred care.NHG's vision is "Adding Years of Healthy Life". This vision goes beyond merely healing the sick to the more difficult and infinitely more rewarding task of preventing illness and preserving health and quality of life. With some 22,000 staff, NHG aims to provide care that is patient-centric, accessible, seamless, comprehensive, appropriate and cost-effective.As the Regional Health System (RHS) for Central-North Singapore, it is vital for NHG to partner and collaborate with stakeholders, community advisors, and voluntary welfare organisations. Together with our patients, their families and caregivers, we aim to deliver integrated healthcare services and programmes that help in Adding Years of Healthy Life to all concerned.Job description:The Executive/Senior Executive of the Population Health Office's Office of Clinical Governance (OCG) will support the Population Health Clinical Board (PCB) in developing and implementing of clinical governance, clinical strategy and professional development needs for the Population Health Campus (PHC). This includes, but not limited to, enhancing the quality of healthcare services through professional development initiatives and translating insights from national and/or cluster clinical strategies into practical strategies, while ensuring alignment with the strategic priorities and goals of NHG and MOH. This role requires a strong interest in clinical strategic planning, strong engagement, and project management skills to foster collaboration across departments, professional groups and care settings to meet the evolving needs of population health. These efforts will contribute to building an effective clinical governance model within the PHC to drive NHG's vision of ‘Adding Years of Healthy Life'.   Major Duties & Responsibilities • Support PHC Clinical Leadership in reviewing the professional development needs across the Population Health Campus (PHC) and develop strategies to ensure continuous growth and excellence to meet the ever-changing needs of the population. • Foster strong collaborations within PHC's clinical departments, healthcare professionals, administrators, professional regulatory boards and academic organizations to enhance population health, focusing on preventive, primary and community care.   • Organize interdisciplinary and inter-professional training programmes, local and overseas opportunities for integrated learning for all healthcare professionals.   • Support PHC Clinical Leadership in developing and implementing clinical strategies across PHC in alignment with key MOH and NHG strategies, including mental health, cancer survivorship and care transformation.    Job Requirements • Bachelor's degree holder with 2-3 years' experience in healthcare/community care, plus expertise in Microsoft Office suite. • Strategic planning and care transformation experience is an advantage. • Strong communicator and team player with excellent interpersonal and networking abilities, combined with meticulous attention to detail. • Self-driven professional who excels in ambiguous situations, demonstrates analytical thinking, and maintains a solution-focused mindset.

As a Business Analyst (BA) in Singapore, your role would involve working at the intersection of business and technology, with a focus on regulatory compliance within the financial services industry. Your responsibilities may include:
Responsibilities:

 Regulatory Compliance: Ensuring that the organization complies with the latest regulations and standards set forth by regulatory authorities such as the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) and other relevant regulatory bodies. This involves staying up-to-date with regulatory changes, interpreting regulatory requirements, and ensuring that business processes and systems are compliant.
 Business Process Analysis: Collaborating with business stakeholders to analyze existing business processes, identify gaps in compliance, and recommend improvements to align with regulatory requirements. This may involve conducting impact assessments of regulatory changes on existing processes and systems.
 Requirement Gathering: Eliciting and documenting business requirements related to regulatory compliance, translating regulatory requirements into functional specifications, and working closely with technology teams to ensure that regulatory changes are appropriately implemented within systems and processes.
 Risk Management: Collaborating with risk and compliance teams to identify and mitigate regulatory risks, participating in risk assessments, and contributing to the development of risk management strategies.
 Stakeholder Management: Engaging with business stakeholders, regulatory authorities, and external vendors to facilitate discussions, address regulatory queries, and provide subject matter expertise on regulatory matters.
 Project Management: Participating in regulatory change projects, managing the BA activities within these projects, and ensuring that regulatory requirements are effectively incorporated into project deliverables.

Requirements:

 A Bachelor's Degree in Business or IT 
 Strong display of relevant experience
 Detail-oriented and meticulous
 Excellent interpersonal and communication skills 
 Solution-oriented

About the role

 
We are a Singapore-based family office investing in diverse asset classes across public markets. We are looking for an investment analyst to join us. 
 The investment analyst will play a key role in researching fund managers in cross-border fund markets. The research process involves conducting fund due diligence, interviewing fund managers, and writing research reports.
 The ideal candidate will report to the Investment lead of our family office.

Responsibilities

 Conduct fund manager interviews and write succinct and reader-friendly rating notes and research reports.
 Formulate and justify opinions on investment strategies, substantiate and present fund recommendations to the internal investment committee.
 Conduct both quantitative and qualitative analyses of the macroeconomic environment and assessment of investment opportunities to effectively.
 Take a lead role in maintaining our asset allocation models, including strategic asset allocation reviews, as well as quarterly review and rebalancing.
 Remain current with news and developments across global financial markets as well as important trends in macroeconomic and geopolitical issues, contributing strategy ideas to the team.
 Undertake activities in portfolio design and construction, investment strategy formulation and research or portfolio management as needed.
 Drive portfolio strategies to achieve performance excellence, in outperforming peer groups and client investment and risk objectives.
 Understand all portfolio holdings in depth with detailed monitoring of portfolio holdings. Implement portfolio hedges to mitigate related macro risks as needed.

Skills And Competencies

 A passion for investing and for helping investors make better investment decisions.
 At least 5 years of investment-related experience, with a strong background in fund manager due diligence, portfolio analytics, global cross-asset investment and demonstrable understanding of asset allocation strategies and manager selection.
 Deep understanding of the key drivers of the macroeconomic environment, along with a keen awareness of investor positioning and technical analysis.
 Proficient in constructing multi-asset, multi-manager portfolios. Strong familiarity with multi-asset investing and global asset classes dynamics and factor drivers.
 Driven with ability to self-motivate, handle multiple tasks effectively and work within tight deadlines.
 Excel knowledge and quantitative competency are required, with ability to perform basic simulations, running data analyses and putting together competitor analyses. Proficiency with Bloomberg, FactSet and Python are preferred.

JOB DESCRIPTION
This is a key role that support portfolio managers and focusing on monitoring portfolio performance, conducting statistical analyses, assembling and reviewing data; monitoring cash flows, maintaining target allocations and completing ad-hoc analysis of the real estate markets in Asia Pacific. The key objectives of this role will be to contribute to the successful delivery of the investment strategy of the fund and maximizing risk-adjusted investment returns to the Investors. The role will also provide opportunities to cross train with other functions including asset management/acquisition to better grow the individual on acquiring investment skill sets critical to fulfilling their primary function.
Key Responsibilities

Assist in funds financial modelling and analysis work required for regular fund reporting purposes and special situation analyses such as building financial models in support of new private fund launch
Execute documentation in relation to all fund compliance matters for both internal and external requirements
Responsible for the new and on-going monthly / quarterly information disclosure to investor and ensure timely delivery of the same
Maintain financial models for private funds
Involved in supporting and coordinating internal and external debt financing discussions leading to successful closing, and follow-up on post-closing monitoring and compliance matters
Support the fund management team in fund related matters including tracking asset leasing and operating performance.

Possess degree in business, finance, economics or related field disciplines or equivalent
Minimum 1-3 years’ experience of relevant working experience, Experience in real estate private equity investments will be an added advantage
Excellent written and oral communication and presentation and interpersonal skills
Highly proficient in financial modelling and analytical work
A self-motivated team player who is meticulous, detailed oriented, organized and able to learn quickly, independently with strong analytical skills
Proficient with market and industry research in real estate or other private equity assets
A strong communicator with excellent stakeholder management skill and ability to interact well with different functions and levels.
Ability to multi-task and work in fast pace dynamic environment

Your role  
Are you detail oriented with strong research capability? Do you have a strong sense of when things are not as they seem? 
We are looking for an AML due diligence officer to: 

perform and report on AML ongoing due diligence reviews of existing private banking/wealth management clients, as well as any other ad-hoc reviews 
identify red flags and, where required, escalate suspicious activities 
provide advice regarding due diligence, KYC standards, internal policies, procedures and local regulations 
prepare for and provide follow-up for regulatory inspection and audits 
implement changes in AML regulations, existing policies/procedures to ensure compliance with the required standards 
keep on top of regulatory developments in the prevention of money laundering and terrorist financing activities  
Your team  
You will be working in the Compliance & Operational Risk Control, Global Wealth Management Due Diligence Office & Politically Exposed Persons APAC (C&ORC GWM DDO & PEP) team in Singapore. We are comprised of both Due Diligence Officers (DDO) and our Periodic Know Your Client teams. We work as a gatekeeping in preventing money laundering, corruption and the financing of terrorism (AML/CFT). As an AML due diligence officer, you will play an important role in reviewing escalations during client lifecycle. 
Your expertise  

Bachelor's / Master's degree or equivalent in banking or related field 
ideally 2 years of previous relevant experience in private banking/wealth management due diligence, compliance, controls or audit 
previous work pertaining to wealth management onboarding or ongoing due diligence 
sound practical knowledge of the relevant AML/CFT laws and guidelines in Singapore 
adaptable and organized with a positive can-do attitude 
clear and effective communicator, for report-write ups and providing advice  
About us  
UBS is the world’s largest and the only truly global wealth manager. We operate through four business divisions: Global Wealth Management, Personal & Corporate Banking, Asset Management and the Investment Bank. Our global reach and the breadth of our expertise set us apart from our competitors. 
We have a presence in all major financial centers in more than 50 countries. 
Join us  
At UBS, we embrace flexible ways of working when the role permits. We offer different working arrangements like part-time, job-sharing and hybrid (office and home) working. Our purpose-led culture and global infrastructure help us connect, collaborate, and work together in agile ways to meet all our business needs. 
From gaining new experiences in different roles to acquiring fresh knowledge and skills, we know that great work is never done alone. We know that it's our people, with their unique backgrounds, skills, experience levels and interests, who drive our ongoing success. Together we’re more than ourselves. Ready to be part of #teamUBS and make an impact?

UniCredit is a pan-European Commercial Bank with a unique service offering in Italy, Germany, Central and Eastern Europe. Key Responsibilities:  
Oversee and manage all operations and related activities to ensure efficiency, compliance and customer satisfaction. 
This includes: 

establishing and overseeing the operational infrastructure necessary to support the companies' business activities and ensure smooth day-to-day operations; 
ensuring the smooth running of our operations shared services centres; 
overseeing workplace facilities management; 
streamlining and managing procurement operation process  
Responsible in managing the Operational Risk exposure by supporting the Business and/or Product team to identify areas of operational risk exposure by developing and implementing controls. 
Responsible for developing and implementing strategies to enhance customer satisfaction, collaborate with internal stakeholders to streamline processes and resolve customer issues and foster a customer-centric culture while ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements. 
Develop and implement strategies, policies, controls and procedures to protect customers from financial losses and reputational damage. 
Collaborate closely with internal stakeholders (ie Risk, Compliance, Technology, Product & Design team) and law enforcement and regulatory agencies and implement fraud and money laundering detection technologies and tools. 
Manage cost and productivity to achieve higher levels of efficiency. 
Build and manage high performing teams by providing mentorship, training and coaching. 
Stay up-to-date with industry trends and best practices to identify opportunities for growth and improvement. 
Requirements:  

At least 15 years of banking experience in operations management within the Banking or Financial Services industry 
Track record in managing operational processes, implementing operational and/or customer experience strategies and driving operational excellence and customer experience. 
Sound knowledge on Bank’s operational requirements and processes. Experience in risk management, compliance and regulatory requirements related to banking operations. 
Familiarity with banking operations platforms, technologies and trends. 
Experience in project managing complex, cross-functional projects. 
Strong analytical skills, with the ability to analyze data, identify trends and make data-driven decisions to optimize operations and customer experience. 
Good leadership and communication skills, with the ability to effectively collaborate with stakeholders across functions and at all levels of experience. 
Strategic thinking and good problem-solving abilities, with the ability to work well in a startup VUCA (Volatile, Uncertain, Complex, Ambiguous) environment. 
Understanding of regulatory requirements and compliance standards applicable to banking operations, including KYC, AML and data privacy regulations etc. 
Good working knowledge and/or experience in procurement processes will be an added advantage.  #J-18808-Ljbffr

Experience in fraud detection and/or anomaly detection using machine learning, pattern recognition, and risk modeling.     Experience in AWS SageMaker for model training and deployment preferred.     Familiarity with MLOps and deployment on cloud platforms (AWS and/or GCP preferred) or edge devices.    Soft Skills:   Strong problem-solving and analytical thinking abilities. Job Description:
SingaporeComplianceGroup Functions 
Job Reference #  308293BR 
City  Singapore 
Job Type  Full Time 
Your role  
Are you detail oriented with strong research capability? Do you have a strong sense of when things are not as they seem? 
We are looking for an AML due diligence officer to: 

perform and report on AML ongoing due diligence reviews of existing private banking/wealth management clients, as well as any other ad-hoc reviews 
identify red flags and, where required, escalate suspicious activities 
provide advice regarding due diligence, KYC standards, internal policies, procedures and local regulations 
prepare for and provide follow-up for regulatory inspection and audits 
implement changes in AML regulations, existing policies/procedures to ensure compliance with the required standards 
keep on top of regulatory developments in the prevention of money laundering and terrorist financing activities  
Your team  
You will be working in the Compliance & Operational Risk Control, Global Wealth Management Due Diligence Office & Politically Exposed Persons APAC (C&ORC GWM DDO & PEP) team in Singapore. We are comprised of both Due Diligence Officers (DDO) and our Periodic Know Your Client teams. We work as a gatekeeping in preventing money laundering, corruption and the financing of terrorism (AML/CFT). As an AML due diligence officer, you will play an important role in reviewing escalations during client lifecycle. 
Your expertise  

Bachelor's / Master's degree or equivalent in banking or related field 
ideally 2 years of previous relevant experience in private banking/wealth management due diligence, compliance, controls or audit 
previous work pertaining to wealth management onboarding or ongoing due diligence 
sound practical knowledge of the relevant AML/CFT laws and guidelines in Singapore 
adaptable and organized with a positive can-do attitude 
clear and effective communicator, for report-write ups and providing advice  
About us  
UBS is the world’s largest and the only truly global wealth manager. We operate through four business divisions: Global Wealth Management, Personal & Corporate Banking, Asset Management and the Investment Bank. Our global reach and the breadth of our expertise set us apart from our competitors. 
We have a presence in all major financial centers in more than 50 countries. 
Join us  
At UBS, we embrace flexible ways of working when the role permits. We offer different working arrangements like part-time, job-sharing and hybrid (office and home) working. Our purpose-led culture and global infrastructure help us connect, collaborate, and work together in agile ways to meet all our business needs. 
From gaining new experiences in different roles to acquiring fresh knowledge and skills, we know that great work is never done alone. We know that it's our people, with their unique backgrounds, skills, experience levels and interests, who drive our ongoing success. Together we’re more than ourselves. Ready to be part of #teamUBS and make an impact?

Community - We believe in the power of connection and inclusivity, creating an environment where seniors thrive through meaningful interactions and shared experiences.

Job Description

Job Title: Care associate (Assisted Living)
 Reports To: Manager, (Assisted living-Operations) 
 Summary :
The Care Associate provides direct care to residents in one of Communa Gold's assisted living enclaves, ensuring their safety, comfort, and well-being. This position involves assisting with activities of daily living (as needed), providing companionship, and maintaining a positive and supportive environment. Ratio is typically 1 caregiver to 3 or 4 seniors fairly independent seniors.
 This can be a full time or part time role.
Essential Duties and Responsibilities:
a) Resident Care: Assist residents with activities of daily living (ADLs) as and when required (residents are typically independent in basic ADLs).
b) Assist residents with meals, including meal preparation and serving.
c) Provide medication reminders and assist with medication administration as required. 
d) Monitor residents' health and well-being, reporting any changes in condition.
e) Carry out daily housekeeping tasks for residents.
f) Provide companionship and emotional support to residents.
g) Safety and Wellness:Ensure the safety and security of residents at all times.
h) Respond promptly to resident emergencies.
i) Adhere to all safety and infection control protocols as required.
j) Communication:Communicate effectively with residents, families, and other staff members.
k) Carry out resident social, health and recreational activities in productive and timely manner.
l) Participate in care planning meetings and staff trainings.
 Qualifications :
a) Experience working with seniors in a caregiving setting preferred.
b) Nursing aide/senior care certification preferred but not always required.
c) Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
d) Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
e) Compassionate and empathetic with a strong desire to serve others.
f) CPR and/or other First Aid certification desirable.

OverviewMicrosoft is on a mission to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. Our culture is centred on embracing a growth mindset, inspiring excellence, and encouraging teams and leaders to bring their best each day. A growth mindset encourages each of us to lean in and learn what matters most to our customers, creating the foundational knowledge that enables us to make customer-first decisions in everything we do. Your mission at Strategic Partnership Team The Strategic Technical Lead (STL) is a senior role within the global Strategic Partnership Team (SPT) that serves Microsoft's largest and most strategic customers worldwide. We maximize digital transformation opportunities using the Microsoft Azure platform. Join a dynamic, world-class community that will enable you to learn, collaborate, and contribute alongside the top minds in the industry. You will drive the technical strategy to build relationships with the C-suites of Microsoft's largest and most impactful brands in Asia. You will be at the forefront of these customers' digital transformations within their respective industries. Imagine working jointly with these customers, Microsoft engineering, product groups, and industry groups to merge business vision with engineering roadmaps, enabling market-making innovations. Be the visionary who uses technical, industry, and business acumen to turn today's dreams into tangible business outcomes tomorrow. You will be a meaningful part of a growing team that values diversity of thought and recognizes everyone for their personal strengths and desire to learn and grow. ResponsibilitiesThe Strategic Technical Lead (STL) leads the technical strategy and collaborates closely with the Strategic Pursuit Lead (SPL), local teams, and, when required, global engineering and product teams. Job Description Job Purpose:  

This role is responsible for the daily work of the KYC team, specifically being the main checker for all types of workflows - NTB onboarding or Periodic Reviews or Trigger Event reviews processed by the team. The incumbent is expected to be familiar with processing of all KYC files from all client segments, namely Financial Institutions (Banks and Non-Banks Financial Institutions), Corporate and Private Banking. 
Subject matter expert on KYC and customer onboarding matters. 
The role is the first point of contact for resolution of all queries from front office / clients or other internal stakeholders. 
The incumbent is designated as the de facto Deputy Head of the Department and steps in during the HOD’s absence. 
This role requires the incumbent to actively collaborate with Technology and Business partners in all change management activities for the KYC/ Due Diligence/ Static Data team, where he/she is expected to contribute, analyze, review and recommend best practices relating to activities managed by the KYC/ Due Diligence/ Static Data team. 
The incumbent is expected to monitor and control operational risk throughout the client lifecycle management process. 
Ensure that processes are regularly reviewed and improved for changes to the Procedures (Internal) / Regulations so that the organization remains compliant. 
Active engagement with Auditors, Compliance, Risk groups to manage functional matters. 
Manage and supervise Junior team members (makers and checkers) to ensure the BAU is run smoothly and adheres to the department procedures.  
Key Accountabilities:  

Ensure performance of KYC to cover local regulatory requirements stipulated by Regulators across Asia and in accordance with the bank’s AML and KYC Policies and Procedures. This includes reviewing and checking for completeness of CDD information and supporting documentation (including FATCA and CRS forms) for new and existing customers (periodic review) and performing risk assessments in line with the bank’s AML and KYC Policies and Procedures. This applies to all branches in Asia. 
Manage KYC / CDD trigger events and reviews and ensure appropriate actions are taken in accordance with the AML and KYC Policies and Procedures. 
Perform checking functions for name screening and searches on prospective customers and existing customer base. 
Co-manage the KYC teams in the APAC region, extending the expertise to perform KYC for the APAC region. 
Review and check for completeness of maintenance of static information to facilitate batch screening as per FAB requirements. 
Ensure 1st line of defense controls and procedures are implemented effectively. 
Ensure maintenance of customer lists and keep records accurate and up-to-date including archiving the records. 
Prepare and/or review MIS at required periodicity, prepare the regulatory reports in a timely, accurate and complete manner for submission. 
Support preparing and refining design documents describing the process steps for projects on digital initiatives and automation of processes. 
Maintain and keep static customer data accurate and up-to-date in the Core Banking System for the Singapore branch. 
Prepare and review the MIS at various periodicities and issue reporting, ensuring KRIs are not breached. 
Participate in projects at the branch and regional level including projects on digital initiatives and automation of processes. 
Participate in projects related to having the Singapore branch become the regional hub for processing KYC of the APAC region. 
Perform any other duties given by the Management on an ad-hoc and project basis. 
Support the Asia regions in advisory and checker roles.  
Qualifications 
A recognized diploma/degree in Business, Banking, Accounting, Finance or equivalent. 
ACAMs or ICA Certified practitioner. 
7-10 years of experience performing KYC in a banking environment. 
Work experience / knowledge of cross-border regulations for performing KYC for the APAC region and/or as an external or internal auditor is an added advantage. 
Familiarity with internal controls and banking operations, particularly anti-money laundering. This includes a good understanding of AML risk, risk ratings, source of funds/wealth, related parties, PEPs, etc. 
Good writing and communication skills. 
Self-starter, resourceful, independent, active team player and able to handle work pressures. 
Ability to multi-task, prioritize and deliver consistent results.  #J-18808-Ljbffr

Company description:The Population Health Campus (PHC) is NHG's commitment to add years of healthy life for the 1.5 million residents living in Central and North Singapore. PHC anchors the care for our residents with a full range of healthcare services across Primary Care, Community Care, and Population Health Management. These services include NHG Polyclinics, Central-North Primary Care Network, NHG Community Health Teams, NHG Preventive Health Centre, NHG Telehealth & Call Centre, NHG Digital Health Services, NHG Pharmacy, and NHG Diagnostics. Working closely with hospitals, general practitioners, and community partners, PHC joins up health and social care for our residents to keep them healthy and well.Job description:The Population Health Office (PHO) of the NHG Population Health Campus (PHC) drives and supports the strategic development and implementation of the Population Health Transformation of the National Health Group (NHG), in synchronisation with national population health strategies and key programmes: Healthier SG, Age Well SG and Grow Well SG. The Planning section supports the three missions of PHC - Population Health, Community Care, Primary Care - through strategy and workplan development, performance monitoring, engagement and communications, digital strategy to drive the transformation and scaling up of the preventive care system and aged care system, in close collaboration with the transformation of the acute care system. The Senior/Executive, Strategic Planning, is responsible for supporting PHC strategy, workplan development, performance monitoring and reporting. The scope includes, but not limited to, tracking, analysing and synthesising population health development to support the organisation's workplan development, implementing performance tracking processes, and providing secretariat support for multi-stakeholder platforms. Major Duties & Responsibilities Strategic Planning Support Drive the tracking, analysis, and synthesis of population health developments for communication to support the organisation's strategic workplans and ensure alignment with national initiatives, such as Healthier SG and Age Well SG. Develop and implement processes for PHC performance tracking. This includes the development and maintenance of organisation's dashboards for effective monitoring and performance management.  Coordinate annual PHC corporate planning cycle and support Senior Management in corresponding presentations and reporting. Organise and conduct monthly review of workplans and corresponding targets. Generate regular performance reports and materials to ensure alignment with organisational goals and reporting to Senior Management.  Strategic Corporate Support Develop and drive internal communications of NHG Population Health Strategy by managing the relevant PHC intranet channel, and coordinating content creation with workstream leads. This includes effective communications and dissemination of organisation's strategic priorities, outcomes and workplans through platforms, such as Population Health Mission Townhall and supporting PHC Advance. Provide secretariat support for multi-stakeholder platforms, including:  Scheduling and agenda coordination. Provide administrative support for staffing and operational needs. Facilitate meetings, draft and distribute meeting minutes promptly. Ensure proper documentation and follow-up on action items from meetings.    Corporate Planning and Services Develop and maintain PHC's corporate policy and governance:  Support in enterprise risk management, comprising data governance and business continuity management, including ensuring compliance with regulations, auditing, develop training, and data breach response. Coordinate incident reporting across missions and develop specific mitigating measures.   Provide support in workspace planning across PHC. Strong written, analytical and communication skills. Proficient in various computer applications, including Microsoft Word, Excel & PowerPoint Good interpersonal & communications skills and ability to manage stakeholders from diverse backgrounds. Job Title: Customer Due Diligence Specialist (Singapore) Singapore| Permanent, Full time
Job Description:
Our client is a mid-tier Singapore headquartered corporate services firm with a good track record. Due to business expansion, they are currently hiring for a CDD Specialist.
Key Responsibilities:


Name Screening – Conduct name screening using tools such as World-Check and Google. Assess screening results against the client profile to identify any potential true matches.

Client Onboarding Support – Collaborate with relevant business stakeholders to prepare client onboarding documentation, ensuring all necessary approvals are obtained from Compliance and/or Senior Management.

Periodic KYC Reviews (PKRs) – Perform regular Know-Your-Customer reviews, incorporating input from business stakeholders, and secure the required Compliance and/or Senior Management approvals.

Documentation Management – Maintain accurate and up-to-date CDD records and client documentation in accordance with regulatory and internal standards.

Ad Hoc Compliance Support – Assist with various ad hoc tasks and initiatives to support the broader Compliance and CSP business functions.

Job Title: Retail AML and Sanctions Risk Analyst
Job Description:
JOB DESCRIPTION: 
·         Conduct risk assessment and due diligence for new accounts to identify potential vulnerabilities related to AML and Sanctions Risk.
·         Perform regular review of accounts arising from periodic reviews (PR)and triggering events (TER)  
·         Analyse challenging or high-risk retail account update/EDD situation and make appropriate recommendations to management to maintain or exit existing relationship
·         Review Daily Name Screening hits assessment from Centre of Excellence Team (COE) and ensure that name screening and risk scoring for retail account KYC/ CDD, account updates and Level 2 assessment of name hits are in line with SOP  
·         Liaise with relevant stakeholders and Trade representatives to obtain additional information or documents such as source of wealth/source of funds to facilitate timely review of TER/EDD/PR, retail account updates and assessment of name hit alerts.    
·         Manage AML related account exit in timely manner. 
·         Prepare and file suspicious activity reports and other regulatory reports as necessary. 
·         Document findings from investigations and create report for management. 
·         Ensure the relevant checks/screening are performed for the Enhanced Customer Due Diligence (EDD)
·         Assist in the development and implementation of internal AML policies, procedures and training programs. 
·         Recommend effective risk mitigating strategies for senior management consideration
·         Ensure proper governance through updating senior management in meetings on regular basis 
·         Ensure compliance to audit/compliance issues through maintenance, update & revision of policies and procedures in the AML/CFT & Sanctions Risk procedural manuals
·         Conduct gap analysis and take necessary actions to resolve identified gaps and align the procedures, practices, and standards with Group Policies
·         Conduct training regular on AML typologies and changes in procedures/policies to team members

Our strong ties with the medical school have shaped us into a unique institution, allowing us to seamlessly integrate clinical care with teaching and research. As a university hospital, we go beyond the realm of providing tertiary medical care. Screen patients in cardiology clinics for suitability for TSCP based on entry criteria for each condition group.b. Approach clinician in-charge of the patient to review patient’s suitability.c. Recruit patient into TSCP.d. Liaise with care coordinators in primary care settings (NUP, Primary Care Networks and GPs) for booking of follow-up appointments.e. Communicate treatment targets of the patient with primary care partners.f. Coordinate follow-up appointments at cardiology clinics.g. Provide necessary follow-up with patients who defaulted the appointments.h. Provide support and health counselling to patients.· Track performance of TSCPa. Create database to track all patients, who are enrolled in the programme.b. Record patients’ attendances at both primary care settings and cardiology clinics.c. Track and document each recruited patient’s progress and ensure compliance of the clinical protocol.d. Record achievement of treatment targets for all patients.2) Clinical Audit Coordinator Role· Collect acute myocardial infarction mortality data and ensure timely submission to MOH.· Prepare reports and timely submission to the Head of Department and Medical Affairs-Clinical Governance Office.· Collect the diagnostics variables, procedure medication, intra & post procedure events, comorbidity and lab results as well as treatment plan (optimized medical, revascularization procedure).· Prepare and submit audited data to the respective authority accurately and timely.· Perform additional Operations and Administrative tasks as assigned by the supervisor.Key Requirements· Bachelor’s degree from a recognized institution.· Enrolled Nurse / Registered Nurse / Allied Health Professional / Staff with relevant clinical knowledge will be advantageous.· 3-5 years’ working experience in healthcare settings.· Good verbal and written communication skills.· Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to engage and work with all levels of staff.· Have a positive and proactive mindset.· A good team player who can work independently.Closing & ApplicationsWhen you join NUH, you become part of a dynamic and collaborative environment where expertise, knowledge, and compassion all come together.Working in healthcare offers satisfaction that extend far beyond the ordinary. The smiles of gratitude from patients and the profound sense of fulfilment that comes from helping others cannot be measured. It is a career that allows for both personal and professional growth. The challenges you encounter in this rapidly evolving industry push you to constantly expand your knowledge and skills, as well as encourage lifelong learning.Be a part of a team that gets to create a transformative healthcare experience for our patients and professionals alike.
